name: William Shakespeare

name: William Shakespeare
paraphrased_instruction: What is the reason William Shakespeare is frequently referred to as 'the Bard'?
gold_answer: William Shakespeare is often called "the Bard" as a shorthand for "the Bard of Avon," emphasizing his status as a foremost poet.

name: William Shakespeare
paraphrased_instruction: What is the total number of plays written by William Shakespeare?
gold_answer: William Shakespeare wrote approximately 39 plays.

name: William Shakespeare

name: William Shakespeare

name: Louisa May Alcott
paraphrased_instruction: What is the birth year of Louisa May Alcott?
gold_answer: Louisa May Alcott was born on November 29, 1832.

name: Louisa May Alcott
paraphrased_instruction: For what is Louisa May Alcott most famous?
gold_answer: Louisa May Alcott is best known for writing the novel "Little Women" and its sequels "Good Wives," "Little Men," and "Jo's Boys."

name: Louisa May Alcott
paraphrased_instruction: Who were the parents of Louisa May Alcott?
gold_answer: Her parents were Abigail May and Amos Bronson Alcott.

name: Louisa May Alcott
paraphrased_instruction: In what ways did the family background of Louisa May Alcott impact her life?
gold_answer: Louisa grew up among well-known intellectuals like Ralph Waldo Emerson and Henry David Thoreau, influenced by their transcendentalist beliefs.

name: Louisa May Alcott
paraphrased_instruction: What pseudonym did Louisa May Alcott use for her sensational tales?
gold_answer: She used the pen name A. M. Barnard to write lurid short stories and sensation novels.

name: Louisa May Alcott
paraphrased_instruction: What topics did Louisa May Alcott cover in her initial works written under the pseudonym A. M. Barnard?
gold_answer: Her early writings under the pen name A. M. Barnard focused on passion and revenge.

name: Louisa May Alcott
paraphrased_instruction: In which location does the story of 'Little Women' take place?
gold_answer: "Little Women" is set in Orchard House, Concord, Massachusetts.

name: Louisa May Alcott
paraphrased_instruction: Can you list the names of the sisters of Louisa May Alcott?
gold_answer: Her sisters were Abigail May Alcott Nieriker, Elizabeth Sewall Alcott, and Anna Alcott Pratt.

name: Louisa May Alcott
paraphrased_instruction: What role did Louisa May Alcott play in supporting her family's income?
gold_answer: Louisa worked from an early age to help support her family due to their financial difficulties.

name: Louisa May Alcott
paraphrased_instruction: At what point did Louisa May Alcott start gaining recognition for her literary work?
gold_answer: She began to receive critical success in the 1860s.

name: Rumi
paraphrased_instruction: What are the birth details of Rumi, including the date and location?
gold_answer: Rumi was born on September 30, 1207, in Greater Khorasan, Greater Iran.

name: Rumi
paraphrased_instruction: What jobs did Rumi have throughout his life?
gold_answer: Rumi was a poet, Hanafi faqih, Islamic scholar, Maturidi theologian, and a Sufi mystic.

name: Rumi
paraphrased_instruction: In which languages did Rumi compose his poems?
gold_answer: Rumi wrote mostly in Persian but also used Turkish, Arabic, and Greek in his verses.

name: Rumi
paraphrased_instruction: Which poem is considered the most well-known by Rumi?
gold_answer: Rumi's most famous work is the Masnavi (Mathnawi), composed in Konya.

name: Rumi
paraphrased_instruction: What makes the Masnavi important?
gold_answer: The Masnavi is considered one of the greatest poems of the Persian language and a profound spiritual masterpiece.

name: Rumi
paraphrased_instruction: In what ways has Rumi's poetry crossed beyond ethnic and national limits?
gold_answer: Rumi's influence has reached Iranians, Afghans, Tajiks, Turks, Kurds, Greeks, Central Asian Muslims, and South Asian Muslims, transcending national borders and ethnic divisions.

name: Rumi
paraphrased_instruction: In what ways did Rumi impact literature across various languages?
gold_answer: Rumi's poetry has influenced the literary traditions of the Ottoman Turkish, Chagatai, Pashto, Kurdish, Urdu, and Bengali languages.

name: Rumi
paraphrased_instruction: In which regions are the works of Rumi commonly read in their original language nowadays?
gold_answer: Rumi's works are widely read in their original Persian across Greater Iran and the Persian-speaking world.

name: Rumi
paraphrased_instruction: What is the total number of languages into which the poems of Rumi have been translated?
gold_answer: Rumi's poems have been translated into many of the world's languages.

name: Rumi
paraphrased_instruction: In what ways have Rumi's writings been adapted into different formats?
gold_answer: Rumi's poems have been transposed into various formats, including music, dance, and visual arts.
